mechanism_narrative: Rapid increases in the federal funds rate signal an aggressive monetary tightening stance by the central bank, which increases the cost of capital and reduces corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.5% in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate indicates a tight labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon. This is an always-on signal reflecting underlying economic health.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of periods where UNRATE is below 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: High and rising consumer price inflation combined with robust retail sales indicates strong consumer demand that is outstripping supply, leading to higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, despite potential monetary tightening.
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation and strong retail sales in 2022
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between CPIAUCSL and RSAFS shows no significant positive correlation with returns.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production signals a contraction in the manufacturing and mining sectors, indicating weakening economic activity and corporate earnings, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 1.0% over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of periods following a 1.0% 3-month decline in INDPRO, or the signal's predictive power (Rank IC) falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in real GDP reflects a healthy and expanding economy, which underpins corporate profitability and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over longer horizons, specifically 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of periods where GDPC1 growth is positive, or the correlation between GDPC1 growth and SPX returns turns negative over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is significantly below the inflation rate, it indicates a highly accommodative monetary policy relative to price pressures, which can fuel asset price inflation and lead to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term shows no significant positive correlation with returns when real rates are deeply negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration or contraction in non-farm payrolls signals a weakening labor market and potential economic recession, which negatively impacts consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth falls below 0.1% MoM or turns negative
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of periods following a significant PAYEMS slowdown, or the signal's predictive power (Rank IC) falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in housing starts indicates a weakening housing market, which is a leading indicator of broader economic slowdown and reduced consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 10% over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of periods following a 10% 3-month decline in HOUST, or the correlation between HOUST and SPX returns turns positive over any 3-year rolling window.
